whatThisIs: Display the ISMP High-Alert Medications list - the drugs and drug classes that carry a heightened risk of patient harm when used in error. The list covers concentrated electrolytes (potassium chloride, hypertonic saline), insulin, opioids, anticoagulants, chemotherapeutic agents, neuromuscular blockers, oral hypoglycemics, and the rest of the categories ISMP has flagged through its medication-error surveillance. Sophie Well shows the class, the named agents, and the standard error-prevention strategies (independent double-check, smart-pump library use, tall-man lettering, removed-from-floor-stock policies).
whenToUse: Use this when designing or auditing a unit-stock list, when reviewing a new medication for inclusion in an automated dispensing cabinet override profile, or when an educator needs the canonical list for a high-alert competency module. The identities of the agents are factual; the institutional policies built around them vary by site. ISMP at ismp.org maintains the authoritative list - this is a fast reference, not a substitute for the source.
